title: Job#process_template mis-escapes options adjacent to mixed quote characters
body: process_template in job.rb only special-cases escaping when the character immediately before and after the `:option` token are both single quotes or both double quotes. When a job's command mixes quoting styles right around a variable (e.g. `"path='#{path}'"` style templates), the option value isn't escaped at all, which can produce a broken crontab line.

title: Cron::REGEX doesn't recognize @reboot combined with role restriction
body: Using `every :reboot, roles: [:app]` in schedule.rb produces output that Cron::REGEX (used to detect an already-valid raw cron expression vs. a comma list) doesn't seem to match consistently once role filtering changes how the string is built, which occasionally causes '@reboot' to be split on commas incorrectly.

title: Precedence between whenever_environment and --set environment= is undocumented
body: If schedule.rb sets `set :environment, 'staging'` via a capistrano-style variable, but I also run `whenever --set environment=production`, which value actually wins when generating the crontab? command_line.rb clearly parses --set overrides but the docs don't say whether they take priority over in-file variables.

title: Capistrano v2 recipe runs whenever:update on every deploy even with no schedule changes
body: recipes.rb hooks `whenever:update_crontab` into `deploy:finalize_update` unconditionally. On a large crontab this adds a noticeable delay to every deploy even when config/schedule.rb hasn't changed since the last release. Could the hook diff against the previous release's schedule file first?

title: Cron binary path detection doesn't account for NixOS/Alpine locations
body: os.rb appears to assume a fairly standard FHS location for the cron/crontab binary. On NixOS and on Alpine-based containers, crontab lives at a different path and whenever fails to find it, even though cron itself is installed and running.
